question: What is the total number of branches of the Akhil Bharatiya Marathi Sahitya Mahamandal?

sources.snippet:
kipedia.org/w/index.php?title=%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%B0%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%A0%E0%A5%80_%E0%A4%B8%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%B9%E0%A4%BF%E0%A4%A4%E0%A5%8D%E0%A4%AF_%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%B9%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%82%E0%A4%A1%E0%A4%B3&action=edit&section=1 "विभाग: दर तीन वर्षांनी बदलणारे कार्यालय संपादा")]
मराठी साहित्य महामंडळाचे व्यवस्थापकीय कार्यालय प्रत्येक घटक संस्थेकडे तीन वर्षांच्या मुदतीसाठी असते. पुढील क्रमानुसार ते फिरते: १. मुंबई मराठी साहित्य संघ. २. महाराष्ट्र साहित्य परिषद, पुणे. ३. विदर्भ साहित्य संघ, नागपूर. ४. मराठवाडा साहित्य परिषद, औरंगाबाद. महामंडळाचे कार्यालय मुंबई मराठी साहित्य संघाकडे १९८६, १९९८ आणि २०१० या साली तीन तीन वर्षांकरिता आले होते. २०१३साली ते पुण्याच्या [महाराष्ट्र साहित्य परिषदेकडे](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/महाराष्ट्र_साहित्य_परिषद "महाराष्ट्र साहित्य परिषद") आले आहे.
इतिहास
------
[[संपादन](//mr.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%B0%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%A0%E0%A5%80_%E0%A4%B8%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%B9%E0%A4%BF%E0%A4%A4%E0%A5%8D%E0%A4%AF_%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%B9%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%82%E0%A4%A1%E0%A4%B3&action=edit&section=2 "विभाग: इतिहास संपादा")]
सर्व मराठी भाषिक मुलुख एका राज्य छ्त्राखाली आणण्याचा विचार राजकीय क्षेत्रामध्ये गतिमान झाला होता. त्या विचाराला बळ देण्यासाठी आणि मराठी भाषा व साहित्य ह्या संदर्भातील समान प्रश्नांची सोडवणूक करण्यासाठी अखिल भारतीय मराठी साहित्य परिषदेसकट वेगवेगळ्या मराठी साहित्य संस्थांना एकत्र आणण्याचे आणि सर्वांची मिळून एक प्रातिनिधिक संस्था स्थापन करण्याचे प्रयत्न सुरू झाले. त्या प्रयत्नांची परिणती १९५१ मध्ये सर्व साहित्य संस्थांच्या प्रतिनिधींची [मिरज](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/मिरज "मिरज") येथे बैठक होण्यात झाली. [कविवर्य अनिल](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/अनिल "अनिल") उपाख्य आत्माराम रावजी देशपांडे यांचा त्यात पुढाकार होता. मिरजेच्या बैठकीच्या अध्यक्षस्थानीही अनिलच होते. या बैठकीत सर्व संस्था मिळून तयार होणाऱ्या संघ-संस्थेच्या घटनेचा मसुदा तयार करण्यात आला. संकल्पित संस्थेला 'मराठी साहित्य महामंडळ' असे नाव द्यावे अशी अनिलांनीच सूचना केली.[[१]](#cite_note-1)
महाराष्ट्र, गोवा, छत्तीसगढ, मध्य प्रदेश या राज्यांत आणि बिदर, बेळगाव इत्यादी गावांत अखिल भारतीय मराठी साहित्य परिषदेच्या ३५० शाखा असून, १२ हजाराच्यावर सभासद आहेत. मराठी साहित्यातील सुमारे ४० हजार कवी साहित्यिकांचे नेतृत्व करणारी ही सगळ्यात मोठी साहित्य परिषद आहे. मराठी भाषेचा प्रचार व प्रसारासाठी ही परिषद काम करीत असून, लोकसहभागातून संमेलने व विविध उपक्रम राबविले जातात.
पहिले काम
---------
[[संपादन](//mr.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%B0%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%A0%E0%A5%80_%E0%A4%B8%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%B9%E0%A4%BF%E0%A4%A4%E0%A5%8D%E0%A4%AF_%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%B9%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%82%E0%A4%A1%E0%A4%B3&action=edit&section=3 "विभाग: पहिले काम संपादा")]
या चारही संस्थांनी मिळून स्थापन केलेल्या या संस्थेचे पहिले अध्यक्ष प्रा. [दत्तो वामन पोतदार](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/दत्तो_वामन_पोतदार "दत्तो वामन पोतदार") हे होते. १९६२मध्ये संपूर्ण महाराष्ट्रात आणि बृहन्महाराष्ट्रात वापरल्या जाणाऱ्या मराठी भाषेच्या लेखनात एकसूत्रीपणा आणण्यासाठी भाषा लेखनाचे काही नियम तयार केले तेच [मराठी शुद्धलेखनाचे नियम](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/शुद्धलेखनाचे_नियम "शुद्धलेखनाचे नियम") होत. हे नियम १९६२ मध्ये तेव्हाचे मुख्यमंत्री श्री. यशवंतराव चव्हाण, यांनी सरकारच्या वतीने स्वीकारले. हे महामंडळाने केलेले भाषाविषयक पहिले काम होय.
स्थापना दिवस
------------
[[संपादन](//mr.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%B0%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%A0%E0%A5%80_%E0%A4%B8%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%B9%E0%A4%BF%E0%A4%A4%E0%A5%8D%E0%A4%AF_%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%B9%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%82%E0%A4%A1%E0%A4%B3&action=edit&section=4 "विभाग: स्थापना दिवस संपादा")]
दरवर्षी महामंडळ आपला स्थापना दिवस साजरा करीत असते. महनीय वक्त्यांचे भाषण आणि दिवसभराचे चर्चासत्र असे त्याचे स्वरूप असते. ज्या मोठ्या लेखकांची किंवा विद्वानांची साहित्य संमेलनाचे किंवा महामंडळाचे अध्यक्ष होण्याची इच्छा नाही अशा लेखकांचा सन्मान व्हावा म्हणून, गेल्या सुमारे १५ वर्षांपासून, अखिल भारतीय मराठी साहित्य महामंडळाने, आपल्या वर्धापनदिनाच्या कार्यक्रमात महनीय वक्ता म्हणून अशा साहित्यिकाला सन्मानपूर्वक निमंत्रित करून त्यांचा सत्कार करण्यास सुरुवात केली. या प्रसंगी एखाद्या वाङ्मय प्रकाराचा किंवा त्या त्या कालखंडातील वाङ्मयावर चर्चा घडवून आणली जाते.
महाराष्ट्रात वेळोवेळी भाषाविषयक किंवा संस्कृतीविषयक जे प्रश्न निर्माण होतात त्या प्रश्नांचा समाजाकडे किंवा शासनाकडे पाठपुरावा करून ते प्रश्न सोडविण्यासाठी महामंडळ प्रयत्न करीत असते.
साहित्य संमेलने
---------------
[[संपादन](//mr.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%B0%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%A0%E0%A5%80_%E0%A4%B8%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%B9%E0%A4%BF%E0%A4%A4%E0%A5%8D%E0%A4%AF_%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%B9%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%82%E0%A4%A1%E0%A4%B3&action=edit&section=5 "विभाग: साहित्य संमेलने संपादा")]
महामंडळाच्या स्थापनेपर्यंत मराठीची जी [साहित्य संमेलने](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/अखिल_भारतीय_मराठी_साहित्य_संमेलन "अखिल भारतीय मराठी साहित्य संमेलन") भरत होती ती पुण्याची महाराष्ट्र साहित्य परिषद भरवीत असे. या संमेलनांना '[महाराष्ट्र साहित्य संमेलन](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/अखिल_भारतीय_मराठी_साहित्य_संमेलन "अखिल भारतीय मराठी साहित्य संमेलन")` असेच तोपर्यंत म्हणले जात होते. साहित्य महामंडळाने काम सुरू केल्यानंतर शक्यतो दरवर्षी एक [साहित्य संमलेन](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/अखिल_भारतीय_मराठी_साहित्य_संमेलन "अखिल भारतीय मराठी साहित्य संमेलन") भरवावे असा निर्णय १९६४मध्ये मडगांवमध्ये झालेल्या ४५व्या [साहित्य संमेलनात](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/अखिल_भारतीय_मराठी_साहित्य_संमेलन "अखिल भारतीय मराठी साहित्य संमेलन") घेतला आणि १९६५ मध्ये हैदराबाद येथे जे ४६ वे [साहित्य संमेलन](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/अखिल_भारतीय_मराठी_साहित्य_संमेलन "अखिल भारतीय मराठी साहित्य संमेलन") झाले ते महामंडळाचे [पहिले अखिल भारतीय मराठी साहित्य संमेलन](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/अखिल_भारतीय_मराठी_साहित्य_संमेलन "अखिल भारतीय मराठी साहित्य संमेलन") होय.
प्रत्यक्षात ते महामंडळाचे पहिले साहित्य संमेलन असतांनाही महाराष्ट्र साहित्य संमेलनाचेच ते नवे रूप असल्यामुळे संमेलनाचा अनुक्रमांक जुन्यावरूनच पुढे असा चालू ठेवण्यात आला आणि पुढील संमेलनांना महामंडळाचे [अखिल भारतीय मराठी साहित्य संमेलन](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/अखिल_भारतीय_मराठी_साहित्य_संमेलन "अखिल भारतीय मराठी साहित्य संमेलन") असे नाव देण्यात आले. तेच आजवर चालू आहे.
अखिल भारतीय मराठी साहित्य परिषदेच्या वतीने नवोदित साहित्य संमेलनाशिवाय राज्यात राज्यस्तरीय व जिल्हास्तरीय अशी बारा साहित्य संमेलने होतात. इ.स.२०१२ पर्यंत झालेली अशी संमेलने :
* राज्यस्तरीय [महात्मा फुले प्रबोधन मराठी साहित्य संमेलन](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/महात्मा_फुले_प्रबोधन_मराठी_साहित्य_संमेलन "महात्मा फुले प्रबोधन मराठी साहित्य संमेलन") : २००७ सालापासून, खानवडी ‍(तालुका पुरंदर, जिल्हा पुणे)
* [छत्रपती संभाजी महाराज साहित्य संमेलन](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/संभाजी_साहित्य_संमेलन "संभाजी साहित्य संमेलन"), सासवड (२००८ सालापासून)
* [ग्रामीण साहित्य संमेलन](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/ग्रामीण_साहित्य_संमेलन "ग्रामीण साहित्य संमेलन") अहमदनगर,
* जिल्हास्तरीय संमेलन नाशिक, सातारा, उस्मानाबाद, श्रीगोंदा, श्रीरामपूर
* विभागीय साहित्य संमेलन वणी (विदर्भ)
* [परिवर्तन साहित्य संमेलन](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/परिवर्तन_साहित्य_संमेलन "परिवर्तन साहित्य संमेलन"), पलूस (सांगली जिल्हा-२००५), अध्यक्ष किरण शिंदे.
संलग्न संस्था
-------------
[[संपादन](//mr.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%B0%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%A0%E0%A5%80_%E0%A4%B8%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%B9%E0%A4%BF%E0%A4%A4%E0%A5%8D%E0%A4%AF_%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%B9%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%82%E0%A4%A1%E0%A4%B3&action=edit&section=6 "विभाग: संलग्न संस्था संपादा")]
१. मराठी साहित्य संघ (मुंबई) -कार्यक्षेत्र मुंबई शहर व उपनगरे. आजीव सभासद २०००. मतदारसंख्या १७५.  
२ महाराष्ट्र साहित्य परिषद (पुणे) -कार्यक्षेत्र कोकण, पश्चिम महाराष्ट्र आणि खानदेश (एकूण १४ जिल्हे). आजीव सभासद : ९०००. मतदारसंख्या १७५.  
३. मराठवाडा साहित्य परिषद (औरंगाबाद) -कार्यक्षेत्र मराठवाडा (आठ जिल्हे). आजीव सभासद २४००. मतदारसंख्या १७५.  
४. विदर्भ साहित्य संघ (नागपूर)-कार्यक्षेत्र विदर्भ (११ जिल्हे). आजीव सभासद ६०००. मतदारसंख्या १७५.
[कोकण मराठी साहित्य परिषद](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/कोकण_मराठी_साहित्य_परिषद "कोकण मराठी साहित्य परिषद") (लघुरूप - कोमसाप)
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
[[संपादन](//mr.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%B0%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%A0%E0%A5%80_%E0%A4%B8%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%B9%E0%A4%BF%E0%A4%A4%E0%A5%8D%E0%A4%AF_%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%B9%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%82%E0%A4%A1%E0%A4%B3&action=edit&section=7 "विभाग: कोकण मराठी साहित्य परिषद (लघुरूप - कोमसाप) संपादा")]
या संस्थेचे रत्‍नागिरी, सिंधुदुर्ग, रायगड जिल्हे, नवी मुंबई, ठाणे जिल्हा आणि मुंबई शहर व उपनगरे हे कोकण मराठी साहित्य परिषदेचे कार्यक्षेत्र आहे. साहित्य क्षेत्रात काम करणारी आणि साहित्य संमेलने भरविणारी ही संस्था अखिल भारतीय मराठी महामंडळाशी संलग्न नाही.
दक्षिण महाराष्ट्र साहित्य सभा, कोल्हापूर
----------------------------------------
[[संपादन](//mr.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%B0%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%A0%E0%A5%80_%E0%A4%B8%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%B9%E0%A4%BF%E0%A4%A4%E0%A5%8D%E0%A4%AF_%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%B9%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%82%E0%A4%A1%E0%A4%B3&action=edit&section=8 "विभाग: दक्षिण महाराष्ट्र साहित्य सभा, कोल्हापूर संपादा")]
साहित्य क्षेत्रात काम करणारी आणि साहित्य संमेलने भरविणारी ही संस्था अखिल भारतीय मराठी साहित्य महामंडळाशी संलग्न नाही.
अनुदान
------
[[संपादन](//mr.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%B0%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%A0%E0%A5%80_%E0%A4%B8%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%B9%E0%A4%BF%E0%A4%A4%E0%A5%8D%E0%A4%AF_%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%B9%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%82%E0%A4%A1%E0%A4%B3&action=edit&section=9 "विभाग: अनुदान संपादा")]
अखिल भारतीय मराठी साहित्य संमेलन आयोजित करणारे अखिल भारतीय मराठी साहित्य महामंडळ व महामंडळाच्या चार घटक संस्था, तसेच वर नावे दिलेल्या पण संलग्न नसलेल्या दोन संस्था यांना दरवर्षी रु.५ लाख इतके अनुदान ’महाराष्ट्र साहित्य आणि संस्कृती महामंडळ’ या सरकारी संस्थेमार्फत देण्यात येते. शिवाय प्रत्येक अखिल भारतीय मराठी साहित्य संमेलनाला सुमारे २५ लाख रुपयांचे अनुदान असते. भारताबाहेर भरलेल्या मराठी विश्व साहित्य संमेलनांनासुद्धा सरकारी अनुदान मिळाले आहे.
### बृहन्महाराष्ट्र
[[संपादन](//mr.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%B0%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%A0%E0%A5%80_%E0%A4%B8%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%B9%E0%A4%BF%E0%A4%A4%E0%A5%8D%E0%A4%AF_%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%B9%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%82%E0%A4%A1%E0%A4%B3&action=edit&section=10 "विभाग: बृहन्महाराष्ट्र संपादा")]
महामंडळाची स्थापना झाल्यानंतर महाराष्ट्राबाहेर असलेल्या ज्या साहित्य संस्था महाराष्ट्रालगतच्या आंध्रप्रदेश, कर्नाटक, मध्यप्रदेश या राज्यांमध्ये काम करीत होत्या, त्या साहित्य संस्थांनी महामंडळामध्ये समाविष्ट होण्याची इच्छा व्यक्त केली. त्या संस्थांच्या इच्छेचा, मूळ चार घटक संस्थांनी गंभीरपणाने विचार केला आणि त्यांना साहित्य महामंडळात समाविष्ट करून घेण्याचा निर्णय घेतला गेला. या निर्णयानुसार [मराठी साहित्य परिषद आंध्रप्रदेश](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/मराठी_साहित्य_परिषद_आंध्रप्रदेश "मराठी साहित्य परिषद आंध्रप्रदेश") ही हैद्राबादची संस्था १९६४ मध्ये पहिल्यांदा साहित्य महामंडळात समाविष्ट झाली. त्यानंतर नजीकच्या काळात [कर्नाटक राज्य साहित्य परिषद](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/कर्नाटक_राज्य_साहित्य_परिषद "कर्नाटक राज्य साहित्य परिषद") आणि [मध्यप्रदेश साहित्य संघ](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/मध्यप्रदेश_साहित्य_संघ "मध्यप्रदेश साहित्य संघ") या दोन संस्थाही समाविष्ट झाल्या. या तिन्ही संस्था समाविष्ट झाल्यानंतर साहित्य महामंडळाचे रूपांतर अखिल भारतीय मराठी साहित्य महामंडळात झाले. मात्र या संस्थांच्या सभासदांना मताधिकार नाही.
महामंडळाचे अध्यक्ष
------------------
[[संपादन](//mr.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%B0%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%A0%E0%A5%80_%E0%A4%B8%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%B9%E0%A4%BF%E0%A4%A4%E0%A5%8D%E0%A4%AF_%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%B9%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%82%E0%A4%A1%E0%A4%B3&action=edit&section=11 "विभाग: महामंडळाचे अध्यक्ष संपादा")]
साहित्य महामंडळाची स्थापना झाल्यानंतर बरीच वर्षे दत्तो वामन पोतदार हेच महामंडळाचे अध्यक्ष होते. काही वर्षांनंतर(?) महामंडळाचे पदाधिकारी निवडणुकीने ठरू लागले. आणखी काही वर्षे गेल्यानतर(?) महामंडळाचे कार्यालय एकाच ठिकाणी ठेवण्याऐवजी ते चारही घटक संस्थांकडे म्हणजे मूळ संस्थांकडे फिरते असावे असा निर्णय महामंडळाने घेतला आणि त्यानंतर प्रत्येक तीन वर्षांनी संस्थांच्या ज्येष्ठतेनुसार पुणे, नागपूर, मुंबई आणि औरंगाबाद या क्रमाने हे कार्यालय दर तीन वर्षांनी स्थलांतरित होणे सुरू झाले.
आतापर्यंत [दत्तो वामन पोतदार](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/दत्तो_वामन_पोतदार "दत्तो वामन पोतदार"),
1. [वि.भि.कोलते](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/वि.भि.कोलते "वि.भि.कोलते")(एक महिन्याकरिता),
2. [कवि अनिल](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/अनिल "अनिल"),
3. [गंगाधर गाडगीळ](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/गंगाधर_गाडगीळ "गंगाधर गाडगीळ")
4. [श्री.पु.भागवत](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/श्री.पु.भागवत "श्री.पु.भागवत"),
5. [राजेंद्र बनहट्टी](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/राजेंद्र_बनहट्टी "राजेंद्र बनहट्टी"),
6. [सुरेश द्वादशीवार](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/सुरेश_द्वादशीवार "सुरेश द्वादशीवार"),
7. डॉ. [सुधीर रसाळ](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/सुधीर_रसाळ "सुधीर रसाळ"),
8. [वसुंधरा पेंडसे-नाईक](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/वसुंधरा_पेंडसे-नाईक?action=edit&redlink=1 "वसुंधरा पेंडसे-नाईक (पान अस्तित्वात नाही)"),
9. [गं.ना.जोगळेकर](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/गं.ना.जोगळेकर "गं.ना.जोगळेकर"),
10. [मनोहर म्हैसाळकर](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/मनोहर_म्हैसाळकर?action=edit&redlink=1 "मनोहर म्हैसाळकर (पान अस्तित्वात नाही)") हे अध्यक्ष झालेले आहेत.
२००७ पासून श्री. [कौतिकराव ठाले पाटील](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/कौतिकराव_ठाले_पाटील "कौतिकराव ठाले पाटील") हे अध्यक्ष होते. १ एप्रिल २०१३ रोजी संस्थेचे कार्यालय पुण्यात आल्यानंतर
* माधवी वैद्य या महामंडळाच्या अध्यक्षा झाल्या होत्या. समहामंडळाचे कार्यालय नागपूरला आल्यानंतर ज्येष्ठ कवी-समीक्षक श्रीपाद भालचंद्र जोशी हे २०१६ ते २०१९ या काळासाठी अध्यक्ष झाले आहेत.
सध्याचे कार्यालय
----------------
[[संपादन](//mr.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%B0%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%A0%E0%A5%80_%E0%A4%B8%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%B9%E0%A4%BF%E0%A4%A4%E0%A5%8D%E0%A4%AF_%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%B9%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%82%E0%A4%A1%E0%A4%B3&action=edit&section=12 "विभाग: सध्याचे कार्यालय संपादा")]
२०१३ सालापूर्वी महामंडळाचे कार्यालय मुंबईत होते. तेव्हा [कौतिकराव ठाले पाटील](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/कौतिकराव_ठाले_पाटील "कौतिकराव ठाले पाटील") संस्थेचे अध्यक्ष होते. मराठी साहित्य महामंडळाच्या घटनेनुसार दर तीन वर्षांनी संस्थेचे कार्यालय एखाद्या घटकसंस्थेकडे जाते. त्याप्रमाणे ते १ एप्रिल २०१३ रोजी पुण्यात आले आहे. तेव्हापासून [माधवी वैद्य](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/माधवी_वैद्य "माधवी वैद्य") या महामंडळाच्या अध्यक्षा झाल्या आहेत. २०१७ साली डॉ. श्रीपाद जोशी हे अध्यक्ष आहेत.
सदस्य
-----
[[संपादन](//mr.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%B0%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%A0%E0%A5%80_%E0%A4%B8%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%B9%E0%A4%BF%E0%A4%A4%E0%A5%8D%E0%A4%AF_%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%B9%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%82%E0%A4%A1%E0%A4%B3&action=edit&section=13 "विभाग: सदस्य संपादा")]
सध्या (इ.स. २०१२) प्राचार्य [कौतिकराव ठाले पाटील](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/कौतिकराव_ठाले_पाटील "कौतिकराव ठाले पाटील") अध्यक्ष, हैद्राबादच्या डॉ. [विद्या देवधर](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/विद्या_देवधर?action=edit&redlink=1 "विद्या देवधर (पान अस्तित्वात नाही)") महामंडळाच्या उपाध्यक्षा, मराठवाडयातील डॉ. [अरुण प्रभुणे](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/अरुण_प्रभुणे?action=edit&redlink=1 "अरुण प्रभुणे (पान अस्तित्वात नाही)") कोषाध्यक्ष तर श्री. [कुंडलिक अतकरे](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/कुंडलिक_अतकरे?action=edit&redlink=1 "कुंडलिक अतकरे (पान अस्तित्वात नाही)") हे कार्यवाह आहेत. पुण्याच्या महाराष्ट्र साहित्य परिषदेचे डॉ. [वि.भा. देशपांडे](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/वि.भा._देशपांडे "वि.भा. देशपांडे"), डॉ. [माधवी वैद्य](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/माधवी_वैद्य "माधवी वैद्य"), श्री. [मधु नेने](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/मधु_नेने?action=edit&redlink=1 "मधु नेने (पान अस्तित्वात नाही)"), मुंबईच्या साहित्य संघाच्या प्रा. [उषा तांबे](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/उषा_तांबे?action=edit&redlink=1 "उषा तांबे (पान अस्तित्वात नाही)"), प्रा. [सुहासिनी कीर्तीकर](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/सुहासिनी_कीर्तीकर?action=edit&redlink=1 "सुहासिनी कीर्तीकर (पान अस्तित्वात नाही)"), श्रीमती [कुंदा पडळकर](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/कुंदा_पडळकर?action=edit&redlink=1 "कुंदा पडळकर (पान अस्तित्वात नाही)"), नागपूरच्या विदर्भ साहित्य संघाचे श्री. [मनोहर म्हैसाळकर](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/मनोहर_म्हैसाळकर?action=edit&redlink=1 "मनोहर म्हैसाळकर (पान अस्तित्वात नाही)"), प्रा. [श्रीपाद भालचंद्र जोशी](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/श्रीपाद_भालचंद्र_जोशी?action=edit&redlink=1 "श्रीपाद भालचंद्र जोशी (पान अस्तित्वात नाही)"), श्रीमती [शोभा उबगडे](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/शोभा_उबगडे?action=edit&redlink=1 "शोभा उबगडे (पान अस्तित्वात नाही)") हे प्रत्येकी तीन सभासद आहेत. गुलबर्ग्याच्या कर्नाटक मराठी साहित्य परिषदेचे [जे.एन.कदम](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/जे.एन.कदम?action=edit&redlink=1 "जे.एन.कदम (पान अस्तित्वात नाही)"), भोपाळच्या मध्यप्रदेश साहित्य संघाचे [अनिल निगुडकर](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/अनिल_निगुडकर?action=edit&redlink=1 "अनिल निगुडकर (पान अस्तित्वात नाही)"), पणजीच्या गोमंतक साहित्य सेवक मंडळाचे [सुरेश नाईक](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/सुरेश_नाईक?action=edit&redlink=1 "सुरेश नाईक (पान अस्तित्वात नाही)"), विलासपूरच्या छत्तीसगड साहित्य संघाचे श्री. [कपूर वासनिक](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/कपूर_वासनिक?action=edit&redlink=1 "कपूर वासनिक (पान अस्तित्वात नाही)") आणि बडोद्याच्या मराठी वाङमय परिषदेच्या डॉ. [वनिता ठाकूर](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/वनिता_ठाकूर?action=edit&redlink=1 "वनिता ठाकूर (पान अस्तित्वात नाही)") हे प्रत्येकी एक एक सभासद आहेत. शिवाय साहित्य संमलेनाचा अध्यक्ष हा पदसिद्ध सभासद असतो. प्रत्येक वर्षात महामंडळाच्या किमान चार सभा होत असतात. आणि या सभांमधून सर्व निर्णय एकमताने किंवा बहुमताने घेण्यात येतात.
महाराष्ट्र शासनाचे आर्थिक साहाय्य
---------------------------------
[[संपादन](//mr.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%B0%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%A0%E0%A5%80_%E0%A4%B8%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%B9%E0%A4%BF%E0%A4%A4%E0%A5%8D%E0%A4%AF_%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%B9%E0%A4%BE%E0%A4%AE%E0%A4%82%E0%A4%A1%E0%A4%B3&action=edit&section=14 "विभाग: महाराष्ट्र शासनाचे आर्थिक साहाय्य संपादा")]
* [महाराष्ट्र शासनाचे](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/महाराष्ट्र_शासन "महाराष्ट्र शासन") वार्षिक ५ लक्ष रुपयांचे अनुदान प्राप्त होते.
* महामंडळाचे [अखिल भारतीय मराठी साहित्य संमेलन](//mr.wikipedia.org/wiki/अखिल_भारतीय_मराठी_साहित्य_संमेलन "अखिल भारतीय मराठी साहित्य संमेलन") भरविण्यासाठी आयोजक संस्थेला राज्य शासनाच्या वतीने २५ लक्ष रुपयांचे अनुदान मिळते. संमेलनाखेरीज महाराष्ट्राबाहेर होणाऱ्या महामंडळाच्या चर्चासत्रादी उपक्रमांसाठी महामंडळ समाविष्ट संस्थांना आर्थिक साह्य करते.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia
Numbers in the Roman numeral system

**This article contains [special characters](/wiki/Help:Special_characters "Help:Special characters").** Without proper [rendering support](/wiki/Help:Special_characters "Help:Special characters"), you may see [question marks, boxes, or other symbols](/wiki/Specials_(Unicode_block)#Replacement_character "Specials (Unicode block)").
[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0e/CuttySarkRomNum.jpg/330px-CuttySarkRomNum.jpg)](/wiki/File:CuttySarkRomNum.jpg)
Roman numerals on stern of the ship [*Cutty Sark*](/wiki/Cutty_Sark "Cutty Sark") showing [draught](/wiki/Draft_(hull) "Draft (hull)") in [feet](/wiki/Foot_(unit) "Foot (unit)"). The numbers range from 13 to 22, from bottom to top.
**Roman numerals** are a [numeral system](/wiki/Numeral_system "Numeral system") that originated in [ancient Rome](/wiki/Ancient_Rome "Ancient Rome") and remained the usual way of writing [numbers](/wiki/Number "Number") throughout Europe well into the [Late Middle Ages](/wiki/Late_Middle_Ages "Late Middle Ages"). Like many other ancient numeral systems, Roman numerals are based on the [additive](/wiki/Addition "Addition") principle: a number is written by concatenating individual symbols, each representing a fixed value, and the value of the resulting numeral phrase is the sum of the individual values of each letter. The modern style of Roman numerals uses only seven letters from the [Latin alphabet](/wiki/Latin_alphabet "Latin alphabet") as symbols: .mw-parser-output .roman-numeral{font-family:"Nimbus Roman No9 L","Times New Roman",Times,serif;font-size:118%;line-height:1}.mw-parser-output .roman-numeral-a{border:1px solid}.mw-parser-output .roman-numeral-t{border-top:1px solid}.mw-parser-output .roman-numeral-v{border:solid;border-width:0 1px;padding:0 2px}.mw-parser-output .roman-numeral-h{border:solid;border-width:1px 0}.mw-parser-output .roman-numeral-tv{border:1px solid;border-bottom:none;padding:0 2px}[I](/wiki/I "I") meaning 1, [V](/wiki/V "V") meaning 5, [X](/wiki/X "X") meaning 10, [L](/wiki/L "L") meaning 50, [C](/wiki/C "C") meaning 100, [D](/wiki/D "D") meaning 500, and [M](/wiki/M "M") meaning 1000. For example, the Roman numeral XXVII represents the number 10 + 10 + 5 + 1 + 1 = 27. When a smaller numeral symbol precedes a larger one, [subtraction](/wiki/Subtraction "Subtraction") is implied; for example, the notation IV represents 5 − 1 = 4 and IX represents 10 − 1 = 9.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia
[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/80/Countries_By_Land_Border.png/250px-Countries_By_Land_Border.png)](/wiki/File:Countries_By_Land_Border.png)
Countries by land border length
[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/01/Borderless_countries.svg/250px-Borderless_countries.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Borderless_countries.svg)
[Antarctica](/wiki/Antarctica "Antarctica") and countries in purple are those without any land border.
This list gives the number of distinct land borders of each country or territory,but not the neighbouring countries and territories. The length of each border is included, as is the total length of each country's or territory's borders.[[1]](#cite_note-Factbook-1)
Countries or territories that are connected only by man-made structures such as bridges, causeways or tunnels are not considered to have land borders. However, borders along lakes, rivers, and other [internal waters](/wiki/Internal_waters "Internal waters") are considered land borders for the purposes of this article.
Land borders
------------
[[edit](/w/index.php?title=List_of_countries_and_territories_by_number_of_land_borders&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Land borders")]
**Distinct land borders**: Refers to the **number of separate geographic boundaries** a country shares with its neighbours. A single country may have multiple distinct land borders with the same neighbour (e.g., due to enclaves, exclaves, or disconnected regions).
**Distinct land neighbours**: Refers to the **number of unique countries** a nation borders via land.

question: At which awards ceremony did the film Oppenheimer, a solemn three-hour biopic that became an unlikely billion-dollar box-office sensation, first win the Best Film award?
answer: 2023 St. Louis Film Critics Association Awards

Since this is a specific recognition and is different from losing an award, runner-up mentions are considered wins in this award tally. For simplification and to avoid errors, each award in this list has been presumed to have had a prior nomination. | |
*[Oppenheimer](/wiki/Oppenheimer_(film) "Oppenheimer (film)")* is a 2023 [epic](/wiki/Epic_film "Epic film") [biographical](/wiki/Biographical_film "Biographical film") [thriller film](/wiki/Thriller_film "Thriller film")[[a]](#cite_note-5) written and directed by [Christopher Nolan](/wiki/Christopher_Nolan "Christopher Nolan"), who co-produced it with [Emma Thomas](/wiki/Emma_Thomas "Emma Thomas") and [Charles Roven](/wiki/Charles_Roven "Charles Roven").[[4]](#cite_note-6) Based on the 2005 biography *[American Prometheus](/wiki/American_Prometheus "American Prometheus")* by [Kai Bird](/wiki/Kai_Bird "Kai Bird") and [Martin J. Sherwin](/wiki/Martin_J._Sherwin "Martin J. Sherwin"), it chronicles the career of American theoretical physicist [J. Robert Oppenheimer](/wiki/J._Robert_Oppenheimer "J. Robert Oppenheimer"), from his studies to his direction of the [Los Alamos Laboratory](/wiki/Project_Y "Project Y"), during [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II"), and the [1954 security hearing](/wiki/Oppenheimer_security_clearance_hearing "Oppenheimer security clearance hearing") that resulted in his political fall from grace.[[5]](#cite_note-7) The film stars [Cillian Murphy](/wiki/Cillian_Murphy "Cillian Murphy") as the titular character with an [ensemble supporting cast](/wiki/Ensemble_cast "Ensemble cast") including [Emily Blunt](/wiki/Emily_Blunt "Emily Blunt"), [Matt Damon](/wiki/Matt_Damon "Matt Damon"), [Robert Downey Jr.](/wiki/Robert_Downey_Jr. "Robert Downey Jr."), [Florence Pugh](/wiki/Florence_Pugh "Florence Pugh"), [Josh Hartnett](/wiki/Josh_Hartnett "Josh Hartnett"), [Casey Affleck](/wiki/Casey_Affleck "Casey Affleck"), [Rami Malek](/wiki/Rami_Malek "Rami Malek"), and [Kenneth Branagh](/wiki/Kenneth_Branagh "Kenneth Branagh").[[6]](#cite_note-8)
*Oppenheimer* had its world premiere at [Le Grand Rex](/wiki/Le_Grand_Rex "Le Grand Rex") in [Paris](/wiki/Paris "Paris") on July 11, 2023,[[7]](#cite_note-9) followed by the British premiere at [Odeon Luxe Leicester Square](/wiki/Odeon_Luxe_Leicester_Square "Odeon Luxe Leicester Square") in [London](/wiki/London "London") on July 13,[[8]](#cite_note-10) and the American premiere at AMC [Lincoln Square](/wiki/Lincoln_Square,_Manhattan "Lincoln Square, Manhattan") 13 in [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City") on July 17.[[9]](#cite_note-11) The film was released theatrically in the United States, the United Kingdom, and most other countries on July 21 by [Un

question: What is the smallest cube number which can be expressed as the sum of two different positive cube numbers in two different ways?
answer: According to the Fermat's Last Theorem, it is impossible for a cube number to be a sum of two cube numbers.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia
Class of integer
.mw-parser-output .hatnote{font-style:italic}.mw-parser-output div.hatnote{padding-left:1.6em;margin-bottom:0.5em}.mw-parser-output .hatnote i{font-style:normal}.mw-parser-output .hatnote+link+.hatnote{margin-top:-0.5em}@media print{body.ns-0 .mw-parser-output .hatnote{display:none!important}}
For number plates assigned to vehicles for hire, see [Taxi medallion](/wiki/Taxi_medallion "Taxi medallion").
[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/21/Srinivasa_Ramanujan_-_OPC_-_2.jpg/250px-Srinivasa_Ramanujan_-_OPC_-_2.jpg)](/wiki/File:Srinivasa_Ramanujan_-_OPC_-_2.jpg)
[Srinivasa Ramanujan](/wiki/Srinivasa_Ramanujan "Srinivasa Ramanujan") (*picture*) was bedridden when he developed the idea of taxicab numbers, according to an anecdote from [G. H. Hardy](/wiki/G._H._Hardy "G. H. Hardy").
In [mathematics](/wiki/Mathematics "Mathematics"), the *n*th **taxicab number**, typically denoted Ta(*n*) or Taxicab(*n*), is defined as the smallest integer that can be expressed as a sum of two *positive* [integer cubes](/wiki/Cube_(algebra) "Cube (algebra)") in *n* distinct ways.[[1]](#cite_note-1) The most famous taxicab number is [1729](/wiki/1729_(number) "1729 (number)") = Ta(2) = 13 + 123 = 93 + 103, also known as the Hardy–Ramanujan number.[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)
The name is derived from a conversation *ca.* 1919 involving [mathematicians](/wiki/Mathematician "Mathematician") [G. H. Hardy](/wiki/G._H._Hardy "G. H. Hardy") and [Srinivasa Ramanujan](/wiki/Srinivasa_Ramanujan "Srinivasa Ramanujan"). As told by Hardy:
.mw-parser-output .templatequote{overflow:hidden;margin:1em 0;padding:0 32px}.mw-parser-output .templatequotecite{line-height:1.5em;text-align:left;margin-top:0}@media(min-width:500px){.mw-parser-output .templatequotecite{padding-left:1.6em}}
> I remember once going to see him [Ramanujan] when he was lying ill at [Putney](/wiki/Putney "Putney"). I had ridden in taxi-cab No. [1729](/wiki/1729_(number) "1729 (number)"), and remarked that the number seemed to be rather a dull one, and that I hoped it was not an unfavourable omen. "No," he replied, "it is a very interesting number; it is the smallest number expressible as the sum of two cubes in two different ways."[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)
History and definition
----------------------
[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Taxicab_number&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History and definition")]
The pairs of summands of the Hardy–Ramanujan number Ta(2) = 1729 were first mentioned by [Bernard Frénicle de Bessy](/wiki/Bernard_Fr%C3%A9nicle_de_Bessy "Bernard Frénicle de Bessy"), who published his observation in 1657. 1729 was made famous as the first taxicab number in the early 20th century by a story involving [Srinivasa Ramanujan](/wiki/Srinivasa_Ramanujan "Srinivasa Ramanujan") in claiming it to be the smallest for his particular example of two summands. In 1938, [G. H. Hardy](/wiki/G._H._Hardy "G. H. Hardy") and [E. M. Wright](/wiki/E._M._Wright "E. M. Wright") proved that such numbers exist for all positive [integers](/wiki/Integer "Integer") *n*, and their proof is easily converted into a program to generate such numbers. However, the proof makes no claims about whether the thus-generated numbers are *the smallest possible* and so it does not directly provide the actual value of Ta(*n*).
The taxicab numbers subsequent to 1729 were found with the help of computers. [John Leech](/wiki/John_Leech_(mathematician) "John Leech (mathematician)") obtained Ta(3) in 1957. E. Rosenstiel, J. A. Dardis and C. R. Rosenstiel found Ta(4) in 1989.[[6]](#cite_note-6) J. A. Dardis found Ta(5) in 1994 and it was confirmed by David W. Wilson in 1999.[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEWilson1999-8) Ta(6) was announced by Uwe Hollerbach on the NMBRTHRY mailing list on March 9, 2008,[[9]](#cite_note-9) following a 2003 paper by Calude et al. that gave a 99% probability that the number was actually Ta(6).[[10]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTECaludeCaludeDinneen2003-10) Upper bounds for Ta(7) to Ta(12) were found by Christian Boyer in 2006.[[11]](#cite_note-11)
The restriction of the [summands](/wiki/Addition#Notation_and_terminology "Addition") to positive numbers is necessary, because allowing negative numbers allows for more (and smaller) instances of numbers that can be expressed as sums of cubes in *n* distinct ways. (sequence [A293647](//oeis.org/A293647 "oeis:A293647") in the [OEIS](/wiki/On-Line_Encyclopedia_of_Integer_Sequences "On-Line Encyclopedia of Integer Sequences")). The concept of a [cabtaxi number](/wiki/Cabtaxi_number "Cabtaxi number") has been introduced to allow for alternative, less restrictive definitions of this nature. In a sense, the specification of two summands and powers of three is also restrictive; a [generalized taxicab number](/wiki/Generalized_taxicab_number "Generalized taxicab number") allows for these values to be other than two and three, respectively.

Cubefree taxicab numbers
------------------------
[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Taxicab_number&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Cubefree taxicab numbers")]
A more restrictive taxicab problem requires that the taxicab number be [cubefree](/wiki/Cubefree "Cubefree"), which means that it is not divisible by any cube other than 13. When a cubefree taxicab number T is written as *T* = *x*3 + *y*3, the numbers x and y must be [relatively prime](/wiki/Relatively_prime "Relatively prime"). Among the taxicab numbers Ta(*n*) listed above, only Ta(1) and Ta(2) are cubefree taxicab numbers. The smallest cubefree taxicab number with three representations was discovered by [Paul Vojta](/wiki/Paul_Vojta "Paul Vojta") (unpublished) in 1981 while he was a graduate student:

Fermat's Last Theorem
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| The 1670 edition of [Diophantus](/wiki/Diophantus "Diophantus")'s *[Arithmetica](/wiki/Arithmetica "Arithmetica")* includes Fermat's commentary, referred to as his "Last Theorem" (*Observatio Domini Petri de Fermat*), posthumously published by his son. | |
| Field | [Number theory](/wiki/Number_theory "Number theory") |
| Statement | For any integer *n* > 2, the equation *a**n* + *b**n* = *c**n* has no positive integer solutions. |
| First stated by | [Pierre de Fermat](/wiki/Pierre_de_Fermat "Pierre de Fermat") |
| First stated in | c. 1637 |
| First proof by | [Andrew Wiles](/wiki/Andrew_Wiles "Andrew Wiles") |
| First proof in | Released 1994 Published 1995 |
| Implied by | .mw-parser-output .plainlist ol,.mw-parser-output .plainlist ul{line-height:inherit;list-style:none;margin:0;padding:0}.mw-parser-output .plainlist ol li,.mw-parser-output .plainlist ul li{margin-bottom:0} * [Effective abc conjecture](/wiki/Abc_conjecture "Abc conjecture") * [Effective modified Szpiro conjecture](/wiki/Szpiro%27s_conjecture#Modified_Szpiro_conjecture "Szpiro's conjecture") * [Modularity theorem](/wiki/Modularity_theorem "Modularity theorem") |
| Generalizations | * [Beal conjecture](/wiki/Beal_conjecture "Beal conjecture") * [Fermat–Catalan conjecture](/wiki/Fermat%E2%80%93Catalan_conjecture "Fermat–Catalan conjecture") |
In [number theory](/wiki/Number_theory "Number theory"), **Fermat's Last Theorem** (sometimes called **Fermat's conjecture**, especially in older texts) states that there are no [positive integers](/wiki/Natural_number "Natural number")

,

{\displaystyle n>2}
![{\displaystyle n>2}](https://wikimedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/44e71ac55b9fbf1e9f341b946cda63d61d3ef2cd) such that

{\displaystyle a^{n}+b^{n}=c^{n}}
![{\displaystyle a^{n}+b^{n}=c^{n}}](https://wikimedia.org/api/rest_v1/media/math/render/svg/1a2e31ced64b8cef38ab186ec86755ecc47c861f).[[1]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEWiles1995443Abstract-1) The cases *n* = 1 and *n* = 2 have been known since antiquity to have infinitely many solutions.[[2]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTESingh202218–20-2)
The proposition was first stated as a theorem by [Pierre de Fermat](/wiki/Pierre_de_Fermat "Pierre de Fermat") around 1637 in the margin of a copy of *[Arithmetica](/wiki/Arithmetica "Arithmetica")*. Fermat added that he had a proof that was too large to fit in the margin. Although other statements claimed by Fermat without proof were subsequently proven by others and credited as theorems of Fermat (for example, [Fermat's theorem on sums of two squares](/wiki/Fermat%27s_theorem_on_sums_of_two_squares "Fermat's theorem on sums of two squares")), Fermat's Last Theorem resisted proof, leading to doubt that Fermat ever had a correct proof. Consequently, the proposition became known as a [conjecture](/wiki/Conjecture "Conjecture") rather than a theorem. After 358 years of effort by mathematicians, [the first successful proof](/wiki/Wiles%27s_proof_of_Fermat%27s_Last_Theorem "Wiles's proof of Fermat's Last Theorem") was released in 1994 by [Andrew Wiles](/wiki/Andrew_Wiles "Andrew Wiles") and formally published in 1995. It was described as a "stunning advance" in the citation for Wiles's [Abel Prize](/wiki/Abel_Prize "Abel Prize") award in 2016.[[3]](#cite_note-abelcitation-3) It also proved much of the Taniyama–Shimura conjecture, subsequently known as the [modularity theorem](/wiki/Modularity_theorem "Modularity theorem"), and opened up entire new approaches to numerous other problems and mathematically powerful [modularity lifting](/wiki/Lift

question: What is the smallest positive integer that can be written as the sum of two perfect squares, with both of these perfect squares being prime numbers?
answer: No square number is a prime number.

**YouTube Music** is a [music streaming service](/wiki/Music_streaming_service "Music streaming service") developed by the American video platform [YouTube](/wiki/YouTube "YouTube"), a [subsidiary](/wiki/Subsidiary "Subsidiary") of [Google](/wiki/Google "Google"). The service is designed with an interface that allows users to simultaneously explore music audios and [music videos](/wiki/Music_video "Music video") from YouTube-based genres, playlists and recommendations. On December 1, 2020, YouTube Music replaced [Google Play Music](/wiki/Google_Play_Music "Google Play Music") as Google's primary brand for music streaming. In April 2023, the service expanded its offerings to include support for [podcasts](/wiki/Podcasts "Podcasts")[[2]](#cite_note-Malik-2) shortly before [Google Podcasts](/wiki/Google_Podcasts "Google Podcasts") was shut down.
YouTube Music also features a premium tier that provides several benefits to paying subscribers. These include ad-free playback, the ability to play audio in the background, and the option to download songs for offline listening. These benefits are also bundled with and available to subscribers of [YouTube Premium](/wiki/YouTube_Premium "YouTube Premium").
History
-------
[[edit](/w/index.php?title=YouTube_Music&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]
The YouTube Music app was unveiled in October 2015 and released the following month; its release came alongside the unveiling of [YouTube Premium](/wiki/YouTube_Premium "YouTube Premium") (originally called *YouTube Red*), a larger subscription service that covers the entirety of the YouTube platform, including the Music app. Although redundant to [Google](/wiki/Google "Google")'s existing [Google Play Music](/wiki/Google_Play_Music "Google Play Music") All Access subscription service, the app was designed for users who primarily consume music through YouTube.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-engadget-ytmusic-4)
On May 17, 2018, YouTube announced a new version of the YouTube Music service, including a web-based desktop player and redesigned mobile app, more dynamic recommendations based on various factors, and Google artificial intelligence technology to search songs based on lyrics and descriptions. In addition, YouTube Music became a separate subscription service (positioned as a more direct competitor to [Apple Music](/wiki/Apple_Music "Apple Music") and [Spotify](/wiki/Spotify "Spotify")), offering ad-free and background/audio-only streaming, and downloading for offline playback, for music content on YouTube. The service's benefits continued to be available as part of the existing YouTube Premium service and to Google Play Music All Access subscribers. The YouTube Music subscription was priced in line with its competitors at US$9.99 per month; the price of YouTube Premium was concurrently increased to $11.99 for new subscribers.[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)
In 2018, YouTube Music reached multiple sponsorship agreements with [Dick Clark Productions](/wiki/Dick_Clark_Productions "Dick Clark Productions") to serve as a partner for its television specials *[Dick Clark's New Year's Rockin' Eve](/wiki/Dick_Clark%27s_New_Year%27s_Rockin%27_Eve "Dick Clark's New Year's Rockin' Eve")* and the [American Music Awards](/wiki/American_Music_Awards "American Music Awards").[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)
YouTube Music became available on [Google Assistant](/wiki/Google_Assistant "Google Assistant") [smart speakers](/wiki/Smart_speaker "Smart speaker") (including [Google Nest](/wiki/Google_Nest_(smart_speakers) "Google Nest (smart speakers)") smart speakers) on April 18, 2019, with feature-limited ad-supported playback available for non-subscribers in only a limited number of countries.[[9]](#cite_note-9)
In July 2024, YouTube Music became available for [Tesla](/wiki/Tesla,_Inc. "Tesla, Inc.") cars.[[10]](#cite_note-10)
Features
--------
[[edit](/w/index.php?title=YouTube_Music&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Features")]
The availability of music encompasses numerous releases from mainstream artists and extends to any video categorized as music on the YouTube platform.
YouTube Music initially operated in parallel with Google Play Music, but the latter was shut down in December 2020.[[11]](#cite_note-11) Product manager Elias Roman stated in 2018 that they aimed to reach feature parity with Google Play Music before migrating users to it, but as of 2024 this has not been achieved.[[12]](#cite_note-12)[[13]](#cite_note-auto-13)[[14]](#cite_note-auto1-14)
In September 2019, YouTube Music replaced Google Play Music in the core [Google Mobile Services](/wiki/Google_Mobile_Services "Google Mobile Services") bundle distributed on new [Android](/wiki/Android_(operating_system) "Android (operating system)") devices.[[15]](#cite_note-15)[[16]](#cite_note-16) In May 2020, an update was released to allow imports from Google Play Music, including purchased music, [playlists](/wiki/Playlist "Playlist"), cloud libraries, and [recommendations](/wiki/Recommendations "Recommendations").[[17]](#cite_note-17)
A "[pre-save](/wiki/Pre-save "Pre-save")" feature for upcoming releases was added in May 2020.[[18]](#cite_note-18)
In 2021, YouTube Music introduced a seasonal recap feature, which serves as a culmination of a user's top artists, songs, albums and playlists, in a similar style to [Spotify Wrapped](/wiki/Spotify_Wrapped "Spotify Wrapped").[[19]](#cite_note-19)
In February 2023, YouTube Music launched Radio Builder, a free and from anywhere accessible web service for both paying subscribers and free users with [iOS](/wiki/IOS "IOS") or [Android](/wiki/Android_(operating_system) "Android (operating system)") devices. It allows users to create a custom radio station, selecting up to 30 artists with an option to hear only their songs or from comparable musicians.[[20]](#cite_note-20)[[21]](#cite_note-21)[[22]](#cite_note-22)
In April 2023, podcasts were added to the service, initially for users in the US only with a worldwide rollout planned at a later date.[[2]](#cite_note-Malik-2)[[23]](#cite_note-23) [Google Podcasts](/wiki/Google_Podcasts "Google Podcasts") was shut down worldwide in June 2024 with YouTube Music replacing it.[[24]](#cite_note-24)[[25]](#cite_note-25)
### Subscriptions
[[edit](/w/index.php?title=YouTube_Music&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Subscriptions")]
The free tier plays songs in its [music video](/wiki/Music_video "Music video") version where applicable. The premium tier plays official tracks of the [album](/wiki/Album "Album") unless the user searches for the music video version. YouTube Music Premium and YouTube Premium subscribers can switch to an audio-only mode that can play in the background while the application is not in use. The free tier does not allow audio-only mode with background playback as it displays video advertisements.[[4]](#cite_note-engadget-ytmusic-4)
The free tier's audio quality supports up to 128 kbit/s, in [AAC](/wiki/Advanced_Audio_Coding "Advanced Audio Coding") and [Opus](/wiki/Opus_(audio_format) "Opus (audio format)") audio formats.[[26]](#cite_note-youtube-format-ids-2024-july-26) When on premium, you have access to 256 kbit/s.
YouTube Music Premium and YouTube Premium plans are available in individual and family variants. A family plan allows up to six family members from the same household to access plan features. Eligible students can obtain a discount on an individual plan.
| Subscription tiers | Ads | Skipping and [scrubbing](/wiki/Scrubbing_(audio) "Scrubbing (audio)") | Offline mode | Background playback | Audio quality |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

| Music Premium | No | Unlimited | Available | Available | All free tier qualities, including 256 kbit/s AAC & Opus[[26]](#cite_note-youtube-format-ids-2024-july-26) |
| Premium | No | Unlimited | Available | Available | All free tier qualities, including 256 kbit/s AAC & Opus[[26]](#cite_note-youtube-format-ids-2024-july-26) |
Geographic availability
-----------------------
[[edit](/w/index.php?title=YouTube_Music&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Geographic availability")]
As of March 2024, YouTube Music is available in most of the [Americas](/wiki/Americas "Americas"), [Europe](/wiki/Europe "Europe") and [Oceania](/wiki/Oceania "Oceania"), as well as parts of [Africa](/wiki/Africa "Africa") and [Asia](/wiki/Asia "Asia"), with a total availability in 119 markets.[[27]](#cite_note-27) Following the [Russian invasion of Ukraine](/wiki/Russian_invasion_of_Ukraine "Russian invasion of Ukraine"), Google indefinitely suspended all of its all payment and subscription-based services in the country, including YouTube Music.[[28]](#cite_note-28)[[29]](#cite_note-29)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia
.mw-parser-output .hatnote{font-style:italic}.mw-parser-output div.hatnote{padding-left:1.6em;margin-bottom:0.5em}.mw-parser-output .hatnote i{font-style:normal}.mw-parser-output .hatnote+link+.hatnote{margin-top:-0.5em}@media print{body.ns-0 .mw-parser-output .hatnote{display:none!important}}
"Bagyo" redirects here. For the city, see [Baguio](/wiki/Baguio "Baguio").
[![Map](https://maps.wikimedia.org/img/osm-intl,3,14.008696,132.495117,294x199.png?lang=en&domain=en.wikipedia.org&title=List_of_Philippine_typhoons&revid=1336493761&groups=_1818c4874b1fc35299ae249510f2c31ef3210a23&parser=legacy)](/wiki/Special:Map/3/14.008696/132.495117/en)
The Philippine Area of Responsibility (red), Tropical Cyclone Advisory Domain (orange), and Tropical Cyclone Information Domain (purple)
**[PAGASA](/wiki/PAGASA "PAGASA")'s Tropical Cyclone Intensity Scale**[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)
| Category | Sustained winds |
| --- | --- |
| Super typhoon (STY) | ≥185 km/h ≥100 [knots](/wiki/Knot_(unit) "Knot (unit)") |
| Typhoon (TY) | 118–184 km/h 64–99 [knots](/wiki/Knot_(unit) "Knot (unit)") |
| Severe tropical storm (STS) | 89–117 km/h 48–63 [knots](/wiki/Knot_(unit) "Knot (unit)") |
| Tropical storm (TS) | 62–88 km/h 34–47 [knots](/wiki/Knot_(unit) "Knot (unit)") |
| Tropical depression (TD) | ≤61 km/h ≤33 [knots](/wiki/Knot_(unit) "Knot (unit)") |
The [Philippines](/wiki/Philippines "Philippines") is a [typhoon-prone](/wiki/Typhoon "Typhoon") country, with approximately twenty tropical cyclones entering its [area of responsibility](/wiki/Philippine_Area_of_Responsibility "Philippine Area of Responsibility") per year. Locally known generally as *bagyo* ([[bɐgˈjoʔ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Tagalog "Help:IPA/Tagalog")),[[3]](#cite_note-amsgom-baguio-3) typhoons regularly form in the [Philippine Sea](/wiki/Philippine_Sea "Philippine Sea") and less often, in the [South China Sea](/wiki/South_China_Sea "South China Sea"), with the months of June to September being the most active, August being the month with the most activity. Each year, at least ten typhoons are expected to hit the island nation, with five expected to be destructive and powerful.[[4]](#cite_note-RapplerMar2016-4) In 2013, *[Time](/wiki/Time_(magazine) "Time (magazine)")* declared the country as the "most exposed country in the world to tropical storms".[[5]](#cite_note-TimeNov2013-5)
Typhoons typically make an east-to-west route in the country, heading north or west due to the [Coriolis effect](/wiki/Coriolis_effect "Coriolis effect"). As a result, [landfalls](/wiki/Landfall "Landfall") occur in the [regions of the country](/wiki/Regions_of_the_Philippines "Regions of the Philippines") that face the [Pacific Ocean](/wiki/Pacific_Ocean "Pacific Ocean"), especially [Eastern Visayas](/wiki/Eastern_Visayas "Eastern Visayas"), [Bicol Region](/wiki/Bicol_Region "Bicol Region"), and northern [Luzon](/wiki/Luzon "Luzon"),[[5]](#cite_note-TimeNov2013-5) whereas [Mindanao](/wiki/Mindanao "Mindanao") is largely free of typhoons. [Climate change](/wiki/Climate_change "Climate change") is likely to worsen the situation, with extreme weather events including typhoons posing various risks and threats to the Philippines.[[6]](#cite_note-6)
[Super Typhoon Yolanda](/wiki/Super_Typhoon_Yolanda "Super Typhoon Yolanda"), internationally known as [Typhoon Haiyan](/wiki/Typhoon_Haiyan "Typhoon Haiyan"), is the deadliest typhoon to have affected the country in recorded history, killing more than 6,300 people as it crossed the [Visayas](/wiki/Visayas "Visayas") region in November 2013. The strongest typhoon to make landfall in the country, as well as the strongest [tropical cyclone](/wiki/Tropical_cyclone "Tropical cyclone") landfall worldwide was [Super Typhoon Rolly](/wiki/Super_Typhoon_Rolly "Super Typhoon Rolly"), internationally known as [Typhoon Goni](/wiki/Typhoon_Goni "Typhoon Goni"), which struck [Catanduanes](/wiki/Catanduanes "Catanduanes") in November 2020 with 1-minute sustained winds of 315 km/h (195 mph). The wettest known tropical cyclone to impact the archipelago was the July 14–18, 1911 [cyclone](/wiki/Cyclone "Cyclone") which dropped over 2,210 millimetres (87 in) of rainfall within a 3-day, 15-hour period in the northern city of [Baguio](/wiki/Baguio "Baguio").[[7]](#cite_note-7) Tropical cyclones usually account for at least 30 percent of the annual rainfall in the northern Philippines while being responsible for less than 10 percent of the annual rainfall in the southern islands. According to the [Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical, and Astronomical Services Administration](/wiki/Philippine_Atmospheric,_Geophysical,_and_Astronomical_Services_Administration "Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical, and Astronomical Services Administration") (PAGASA) in 2016, the number of destructive typhoons the country experienced annually have increased, but notes that it is too early to call it a trend.[[4]](#cite_note-RapplerMar2016-4)
PAGASA is the state weather agency of the Philippines. Yearly, the agency gives a local name to the typhoons that enter its area of responsibility in addition to the international name given by the [Japan Meteorological Agency](/wiki/Japan_Meteorological_Agency "Japan Meteorological Agency") (JMA), the designated [Regional Specialized Meteorological Center](/wiki/Regional_Specialized_Meteorological_Center "Regional Specialized Meteorological Center") (RSMC) by the [World Meteorological Organization](/wiki/World_Meteorological_Organization "World Meteorological Organization") (WMO). The state agency also regularly issues weather bulletins and advisories to the public especially during typhoons. It uses a [five-point warning scale](/wiki/Tropical_Cyclone_Warning_Signals "Tropical Cyclone Warning Signals") that are issued to the entirety or parts of the [provinces and localities](/wiki/Provinces_of_the_Philippines "Provinces of the Philippines") affected by a typhoon.[[8]](#cite_note-AOML_FAQ_B2-8)[[9]](#cite_note-Philippine_Atmospheric,_Geophysical_and_Astronomical_Services_Administration_PAGASA-9)
The [National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council](/wiki/National_Disaster_Risk_Reduction_and_Management_Council "National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council") (NDRRMC) is the country's top agency for preparation and response to calamities and natural disasters, including typhoons. Additionally, each province and [local government units](/wiki/Local_government_in_the_Philippines "Local government in the Philippines") has their own [Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office](/wiki/Disaster_Risk_Reduction_and_Management_Office "Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office") (DRRMO). Each provincial and local government is required to set aside 5% of its annual budget for disaster risk reduction, preparations, and response.[[4]](#cite_note-RapplerMar2016-4)
The frequency of typhoons in the Philippines have made typhoons a significant part of everyday ancient and modern Filipino culture.[[5]](#cite_note-TimeNov2013-5)
Etymology
---------
[[edit](/w/index.php?title=List_of_Philippine_typhoons&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Etymology")]
*Bagyo* (sometimes spelled *bagyu* or *bagyio*[[3]](#cite_note-amsgom-baguio-3)) is the word for 'typhoon' or 'storm' in most [Philippine languages](/wiki/Philippine_languages "Philippine languages"), including [Tagalog](/wiki/Tagalog_language "Tagalog language"), [Visayan](/wiki/Visayan_languages "Visayan languages"), [Ilocano](/wiki/Ilocano_language "Ilocano language"), [Bicolano](/wiki/Bicol_language "Bicol language"), [Hanunó'o](/wiki/Hanun%C3%B3%27o_language "Hanunó'o language"), [Aklanon](/wiki/Aklanon_language "Aklanon language"), [Pangasinan](/wiki/Pangasinan_language "Pangasinan language") and [Kapampangan](/wiki/Kapampangan_language "Kapampangan language"). It is derived from [Proto-Austronesian](/wiki/Proto-Austronesian_language "Proto-Austronesian language") \**baRiuS*, meaning 'typhoon'. [Cognates](/wiki/Cognate "Cognate") in other [Austronesian languages](/wiki/Austronesian_languages "Austronesian languages") include [Sama](/wiki/Sama_language "Sama language") *baliw* ('wind'), [Amis](/wiki/Amis_language "Amis language") *faliyos* or *farios* ('typhoon'); [Saisiyat](/wiki/Saisiyat_language "Saisiyat language") *balosh* ('typhoon'), [Babuza](/wiki/Babuza_language "Babuza language") *bayus* ('storm'), [Puyuma](/wiki/Puyuma_language "Puyuma language") *variw*, [Bintulu](/wiki/Bintulu_language "Bintulu language") *bauy* ('wind'), [Kelabit](/wiki/Kelabit_language "Kelabit language") *bariw* ('storm wind'), and [Chamorro](/wiki/Chamorro_language "Chamorro language") *pakyo* ('typhoon').[[10]](#cite_note-truss-10)
Storm naming conventions
------------------------
[[edit](/w/index.php?title=List_of_Philippine_typhoons&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Storm naming conventions")]
[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e3/2008frank_path_01.jpg/250px-2008frank_path_01.jpg)](/wiki/File:2008frank_path_01.jpg)
Map of the path of [Typhoon Fengshen (Frank)](/wiki/Typhoon_Fengshen_(2008) "Typhoon Fengshen (2008)"), showing it making landfall in the Eastern Visayas before taking a northwesterly path
The [Joint Typhoon Warning Center](/wiki/Joint_Typhoon_Warning_Center "Joint Typhoon Warning Center") in Honolulu started monitoring and naming storms in the Western Pacific region in [1945](/wiki/1945_Pacific_typhoon_season "1945 Pacific typhoon season"), originally using female names in English alphabetical order. That list was revised in [1979](/wiki/1979_Pacific_typhoon_season "1979 Pacific typhoon season") by introducing male names to be used in alternation with the female names.[[11]](#cite_note-11) The Philippine Weather Bureau started naming storms within their area of responsibility in 1963, using female Filipino names in the former native alphabetical order. The Bureau continued to monitor typhoons until the agency's abolition in 1972, after which its duties were transferred to the newly established [PAGASA](/wiki/PAGASA "PAGASA"). This often resulted in a Western Pacific cyclone carrying two names: an international name and a local name used within the Philippines. This two-name scheme is still followed to this day.
In [2000](/wiki/2000_Pacific_typhoon_season "2000 Pacific typhoon season"), cyclone monitoring duties in the Western Pacific were transferred from the JTWC to the [Japan Meteorological Agency](/wiki/Japan_Meteorological_Agency "Japan Meteorological Agency"), the [RSMC](/wiki/Regional_Specialized_Meteorological_Center "Regional Specialized Meteorological Center") of the [World Meteorological Organization](/wiki/World_Meteorological_Organization "World Meteorological Organization"). The international naming scheme of the typhoons was replaced with a sequential list of names contributed by 14 nations in the region, including the Philippines. The new scheme largely uses terms for local features of the contributing nation, such as animals, plants, foods and adjectives in the native language. The rotation of names is based on the alphabetical order of the contributing nations. The Philippines, however, would maintain its own naming scheme for its local forecasts. In [2001](/wiki/2001_Pacific_typhoon_season "2001 Pacific typhoon season"), PAGASA revised its naming scheme to contain longer annual lists with a more mixed set of names.
Currently, the JMA and PAGASA each assign names to typhoons that form within or enter the Philippine Area of Responsibility. The JMA naming scheme for international use contains 140 names described above. The list is not restricted by year; the first name to be used in a typhoon season is the name after the last-named cyclone of the preceding season.[[12]](#cite_note-WMONames-12) The PAGASA naming scheme for Philippine use contains four lists, each containing twenty-five names arranged in alphabetical order. Every typhoon season begins with the first name in the assigned list, and the rolls of names are each reused every four years. An auxiliary list of ten names is used when the main list in a year had been exhausted.[[13]](#cite_note-13) Not all Western Pacific cyclones are given names by both weather agencies, as JMA does not name tropical depressions, and PAGASA does not name cyclones outside the Philippine Area of Responsibility.
In the case of both weather agencies, [names are retired](/wiki/List_of_retired_Pacific_typhoon_names "List of retired Pacific typhoon names") after a typhoon that carried it caused severe or costly damage and loss of life. Retirement is decided by the agencies' committees, although [in PAGASA's case](/wiki/List_of_retired_Philippine_typhoon_names "List of retired Philippine typhoon names"), names are routinely retired when the cyclone caused at least 300 deaths or ₱1 billion in damage in the Philippines. Retired names are replaced with another name for the next rotation, for JMA by the nation that submitted the retired name, and for PAGASA with a name sharing the same first letter as the retired name.
Variability in activity
-----------------------
[[edit](/w/index.php?title=List_of_Philippine_typhoons&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Variability in activity")]
[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/6f/Tropical_cyclones_1945_2006_wikicolor.png/250px-Tropical_cyclones_1945_2006_wikicolor.png)](/wiki/File:Tropical_cyclones_1945_2006_wikicolor.png)
Tracks of tropical cyclones worldwide, 1945–2006. The Philippines is under the red and yellow tracks northeast of [Borneo](/wiki/Borneo "Borneo").
On an annual time scale, activity reaches a minimum in May, before increasing steadily to June, and spiking from July to September, with August being the most active month for tropical cyclones in the Philippines. Activity reduces significantly in October.[[14]](#cite_note-14) The most active season, since 1945, for tropical cyclone strikes on the island archipelago was [1993](/wiki/1993_Pacific_typhoon_season "1993 Pacific typhoon season") when nineteen tropical cyclones moved through the country (though there were 36 storms that were named by PAGASA).[[15]](#cite_note-15) There was only one tropical cyclone which moved through the Philippines in [1958](/wiki/1958_Pacific_typhoon_season "1958 Pacific typhoon season").[[16]](#cite_note-16) The most frequently impacted areas of the Philippines by tropical cyclones are northern Luzon and eastern Visayas.[[17]](#cite_note-17) A ten-year average of satellite determined precipitation showed that at least 30 percent of the annual rainfall in the northern Philippines could be traced to tropical cyclones, while the southern islands receive less than 10 percent of their annual rainfall from tropical cyclones.[[18]](#cite_note-18)
Warnings
--------
[[edit](/w/index.php?title=List_of_Philippine_typhoons&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Warnings")]
Main article: [Tropical Cyclone Wind Signals](/wiki/Tropical_Cyclone_Wind_Signals "Tropical Cyclone Wind Signals")
**[PAGASA](/wiki/PAGASA "PAGASA")'s  
Tropical Cyclone Wind Signals (TCWS)**[[19]](#cite_note-19)
| Warning Signal | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| --- | |
| TCWS #1 | winds of ***39–61 km/h (21–33 kn; 24–38 mph)*** are prevailing or expected to occur ***within 36 hours*** |
| TCWS #2 | winds of ***62–88 km/h (33–48 kn; 39–55 mph)*** are prevailing or expected to occur ***within 24 hours*** |
| TCWS #3 | winds of ***89–117 km/h (48–63 kn; 55–73 mph)*** are prevailing or expected to occur ***within 18 hours*** |
| TCWS #4 | winds of ***118–184 km/h (64–99 kn; 73–114 mph)*** are prevailing or expected to occur ***within 12 hours*** |
| TCWS #5 | winds of ***185 km/h (100 kn; 115 mph)*** or greater are prevailing or expected to occur ***within 12 hours*** |
PAGASA releases typhoon warnings to the public. Until recently, the warning scale it used was a four-point scale, with Signal #4 being the highest possible warning issued to a locality. However, a fifth warning signal was introduced in the 2010s for powerful typhoons since [Typhoon Haiyan (Yolanda)](/wiki/Typhoon_Haiyan_(Yolanda) "Typhoon Haiyan (Yolanda)") in [2013](/wiki/2013_Pacific_typhoon_season "2013 Pacific typhoon season"). In 2022, PAGASA revised its own definition for a "super typhoon" and its warning signals.[[20]](#cite_note-20)[[9]](#cite_note-Philippine_Atmospheric,_Geophysical_and_Astronomical_Services_Administration_PAGASA-9) An area having a storm signal may be under:
* **Signal #1** – Tropical cyclone winds of 39 km/h (24 mph) to 61 km/h (38 mph) are expected within the next 36 hours. If a tropical cyclone forms very close to the area, then a shorter lead time is seen on the warning bulletin.
* **Signal #2** – Tropical cyclone winds of 62 km/h (39 mph) to 88 km/h (55 mph) are expected within the next 24 hours.
* **Signal #3** – Tropical cyclone winds of 89 km/h (55 mph) to 117 km/h (73 mph) are expected within the next 18 hours.
* **Signal #4** – Tropical cyclone winds of 118 km/h (73 mph) to 184 km/h (114 mph) are expected within 12 hours.
* **Signal #5** – Tropical cyclone winds of 185 km/h (115 mph) or greater are expected within 12 hours.
These warning signals are usually raised when a locality is about to be hit by a typhoon. As it gains strength and/or gets nearer to an area having a storm signal, the warning may be upgraded to a higher one for that particular area. Conversely, as a tropical cyclone weakens and/or gets farther to an area, it may be downgraded to a lower signal or may be lifted altogether.
Classes in the localities that are under a warning signal are cancelled or suspended depending on how high the signal is: [preschool](/wiki/Preschool "Preschool") for Signal #1, [elementary](/wiki/Elementary_education "Elementary education") and below for Signal #2, [high school](/wiki/High_school "High school") (including [senior high school](/wiki/Senior_high_school "Senior high school")) and below for Signal #3, and all [educational levels](/wiki/Education_in_the_Philippines "Education in the Philippines") (including [colleges](/wiki/Colleges "Colleges") and [universities](/wiki/Universities "Universities")) for Signal #4 and above. These restrictions apply for both public and private schools in the affected locality, although local governments can declare suspensions and cancellations of classes at their own discretion regardless of the warning signal in force.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/37/African_Bush_Elephant.jpg/120px-African_Bush_Elephant.jpg)](/wiki/File:African_Bush_Elephant.jpg)
[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1c/Anim1754_-_Flickr_-_NOAA_Photo_Library.jpg/250px-Anim1754_-_Flickr_-_NOAA_Photo_Library.jpg)](/wiki/File:Anim1754_-_Flickr_-_NOAA_Photo_Library.jpg)
[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/40/Colossal_squid_at_Te_Papa.jpg/330px-Colossal_squid_at_Te_Papa.jpg)](/wiki/File:Colossal_squid_at_Te_Papa.jpg)
Clockwise from top left: an [African bush elephant](/wiki/African_bush_elephant "African bush elephant"), the largest [extant](/wiki/Extant_taxon "Extant taxon") terrestrial animal; a [blue whale](/wiki/Blue_whale "Blue whale"), the largest animal ever to exist; and a [colossal squid](/wiki/Colossal_squid "Colossal squid"), the largest invertebrate
The largest animal currently alive, as well as the largest animal to ever exist, is the [blue whale](/wiki/Blue_whale "Blue whale"). The maximum recorded weight was 190 [tonnes](/wiki/Tonne "Tonne") (209 short tons) for a specimen measuring 27.6 metres (91 ft), whereas longer ones, up to 33.6 metres (110 ft), have been recorded but not weighed.[[1]](#cite_note-Wood-1)[[2]](#cite_note-Lockyer_1976-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-McClain_2015-5)[[6]](#cite_note-AUS2002-6) It is estimated that this individual could have a mass of 250 [tonnes](/wiki/Tonne "Tonne") or more.[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9) The longest non-[colonial animal](/wiki/Colonial_animal "Colonial animal") is the [lion's mane jellyfish](/wiki/Lion%27s_mane_jellyfish "Lion's mane jellyfish") (120 ft, 36.6 m).[[5]](#cite_note-McClain_2015-5)
While controversial, estimates for the weight of the [sauropod](/wiki/Sauropod "Sauropod") *[Bruhathkayosaurus](/wiki/Bruhathkayosaurus "Bruhathkayosaurus")* suggest it was around 110–170 tons, with the highest estimate being 240 tons, if scaled with *[Patagotitan](/wiki/Patagotitan "Patagotitan")*, although actual fossil remains no longer exist, and that estimation is based on described dimensions in 1987.[[10]](#cite_note-Bruhathkayosaurus2023-10) Similarly, the extinct whale *[Perucetus](/wiki/Perucetus "Perucetus")* was initially estimated in 2023 to have a mass of 85 to 340 t (94–375 short tons; 84–335 long tons).[[11]](#cite_note-BianucciEtAl-11) However, more recent studies suggest this whale was much smaller than previous estimates, with a 2024 putting its weight at 60 to 113 tonnes,[[12]](#cite_note-Lve_Science-12) while a 2025 study further downsized its weight to 35 to 40 tonnes.[[13]](#cite_note-:2-13)
In April 2024, *[Ichthyotitan severnensis](/wiki/Ichthyotitan_severnensis "Ichthyotitan severnensis")* was established as a valid [shastasaurid](/wiki/Shastasaurid "Shastasaurid") taxon and is considered both the largest marine reptile ever discovered and the largest macropredator ever discovered. The Lilstock specimen was estimated to be around 26 metres (85 ft) whilst the Aust specimen was an even more impressive 30 to 35 metres (98 to 115 ft) in length (however, the Aust specimen or 'Aust Colossus' may not belong to Ichthyotitan).[[14]](#cite_note-14) While no weight estimates have been made as of yet, *Ichthyotitan* would have easily rivalled or surpassed the blue whale.[[15]](#cite_note-SciNews2018-15)[[16]](#cite_note-NewsScientist-16) The upper estimates of weight for these prehistoric animals would have easily rivaled or exceeded the largest rorquals and sauropods.[[17]](#cite_note-17) However, an as-yet undescribed shastasaurid, informally referred to as ‘[Aust Colossus](/wiki/Aust_Colossus "Aust Colossus")’, may have exceeded Ichthyotitan in size, potentially making it the largest known marine reptile. It has been estimated at approximately 30 metres in length, which would surpass the blue whale; however, the authors note that this estimate is highly speculative.[[18]](#cite_note-18)
The [African bush elephant](/wiki/African_bush_elephant "African bush elephant") (*Loxodonta africana*) is the largest living land animal. A native of various open habitats in sub-Saharan Africa, males weigh about 6 tonnes (13,000 lb) on average.[[19]](#cite_note-probos_mass-19)[[20]](#cite_note-20) The largest elephant ever recorded was shot in [Angola](/wiki/Angola "Angola") in 1974. It was a male measuring 10.67 metres (35.0 ft) from trunk to tail and 4.17 metres (13.7 ft) lying on its side in a projected line from the highest point of the shoulder, to the base of the forefoot, indicating a standing shoulder height of 3.96 metres (13.0 ft). This male had a computed weight of 10.4 to 12.25 tonnes.[[1]](#cite_note-Wood-1)[[21]](#cite_note-21)[[19]](#cite_note-probos_mass-19)
Heaviest living animals
-----------------------
[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Largest_and_heaviest_animals&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Heaviest living animals")]
[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/35/Marine_megafauna_Infographic.jpg/500px-Marine_megafauna_Infographic.jpg)](/wiki/File:Marine_megafauna_Infographic.jpg)
[Infographic](/wiki/Infographic "Infographic") showing the size of marine [megafauna](/wiki/Megafauna "Megafauna")
[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/06/Blue_Whale_001_body_bw.jpg/250px-Blue_Whale_001_body_bw.jpg)](/wiki/File:Blue_Whale_001_body_bw.jpg)
The [blue whale](/wiki/Blue_whale "Blue whale") is the heaviest living animal.
The heaviest living animals are all [whales](/wiki/Whale "Whale"). There are considerable obstacles to overcome in obtaining accurate weights for them, however, as no scale can accommodate the whole body of a large whale. Whaling factories are usually enlisted to help accomplish the task, but it still remains difficult, and most weighings have been done by parts of flensed whales, leaving much room for error. Mathematical formulas have also been devised to estimate the average weight of various whale species based on body length, but this method is equally prone to inaccuracy.[[22]](#cite_note-22)

Heaviest terrestrial animals
----------------------------
[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Largest_and_heaviest_animals&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Heaviest terrestrial animals")]
The heaviest land animals are all mammals. The African elephant is now listed as two species, the [African bush elephant](/wiki/African_bush_elephant "African bush elephant") and the [African forest elephant](/wiki/African_forest_elephant "African forest elephant"), as they are now generally considered to be.[[40]](#cite_note-40)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia
Combining capacity of elements with other atoms
.mw-parser-output .hatnote{font-style:italic}.mw-parser-output div.hatnote{padding-left:1.6em;margin-bottom:0.5em}.mw-parser-output .hatnote i{font-style:normal}.mw-parser-output .hatnote+link+.hatnote{margin-top:-0.5em}@media print{body.ns-0 .mw-parser-output .hatnote{display:none!important}}
For other uses, see [Valence (disambiguation)](/wiki/Valence_(disambiguation) "Valence (disambiguation)").
Not to be confused with [coordination number](/wiki/Coordination_number "Coordination number"), [oxidation state](/wiki/Oxidation_state "Oxidation state"), or [valence electron](/wiki/Valence_electron "Valence electron").
See also: [Polyvalency (chemistry)](/wiki/Polyvalency_(chemistry) "Polyvalency (chemistry)")
In [chemistry](/wiki/Chemistry "Chemistry"), the **valence** (US spelling) or **valency** (British spelling) of an [atom](/wiki/Atom "Atom") is a measure of its combining capacity with other atoms when it forms [chemical compounds](/wiki/Chemical_compound "Chemical compound") or [molecules](/wiki/Molecule "Molecule"). Valence is generally understood to be the number of [chemical bonds](/wiki/Chemical_bond "Chemical bond") that each atom of a given [chemical element](/wiki/Chemical_element "Chemical element") typically forms. [Double bonds](/wiki/Double_bond "Double bond") are considered to be two bonds, [triple bonds](/wiki/Triple_bond "Triple bond") to be three, [quadruple bonds](/wiki/Quadruple_bond "Quadruple bond") to be four, [quintuple bonds](/wiki/Quintuple_bond "Quintuple bond") to be five and [sextuple bonds](/wiki/Sextuple_bond "Sextuple bond") to be six. In most compounds, the valence of [hydrogen](/wiki/Hydrogen "Hydrogen") is 1, of [oxygen](/wiki/Oxygen "Oxygen") is 2, of [nitrogen](/wiki/Nitrogen "Nitrogen") is 3, and of [carbon](/wiki/Carbon "Carbon") is 4. Valence is not to be confused with the related concepts of the [coordination number](/wiki/Coordination_number "Coordination number"), the [oxidation state](/wiki/Oxidation_state "Oxidation state"), or the number of [valence electrons](/wiki/Valence_electron "Valence electron") for a given atom.
Description
-----------
The valence is the combining capacity of an atom of a given element, determined by the number of hydrogen atoms that it combines with. In [methane](/wiki/Methane "Methane"), carbon has a valence of 4; in [ammonia](/wiki/Ammonia "Ammonia"), nitrogen has a valence of 3; in water, oxygen has a valence of 2; and in hydrogen chloride, chlorine has a valence of 1. Chlorine, as it has a valence of one, can be substituted for hydrogen in many compounds. Phosphorus has a valence 3 in [phosphine](/wiki/Phosphine "Phosphine") (.mw-parser-output .template-chem2-su{display:inline-block;font-size:80%;line-height:1;vertical-align:-0.35em}.mw-parser-output .template-chem2-su>span{display:block;text-align:left}.mw-parser-output sub.template-chem2-sub{font-size:80%;vertical-align:-0.35em}.mw-parser-output sup.template-chem2-sup{font-size:80%;vertical-align:0.65em}PH3) and a valence of 5 in [phosphorus pentachloride](/wiki/Phosphorus_pentachloride "Phosphorus pentachloride") (PCl5), which shows that an element may exhibit more than one valence. The [structural formula](/wiki/Structural_formula "Structural formula") of a compound represents the connectivity of the atoms, with lines drawn between two atoms to represent bonds.[[1]](#cite_note-Partington-1) The two tables below show examples of different compounds, their structural formulas, and the valences for each element of the compound.

Definition
----------
Valence is defined by the [IUPAC](/wiki/International_Union_of_Pure_and_Applied_Chemistry "International Union of Pure and Applied Chemistry") as:[[2]](#cite_note-goldbook_valence-2)
:   *The maximum number of univalent atoms (originally hydrogen or chlorine atoms) that may combine with an atom of the element under consideration, or with a fragment, or for which an atom of this element can be substituted.*
An alternative modern description is:[[3]](#cite_note-3)
:   *The number of hydrogen atoms that can combine with an element in a binary hydride or twice the number of oxygen atoms combining with an element in its oxide or oxides.*
This definition differs from the IUPAC definition as an element can be said to have more than one valence.
Historical development
----------------------
The [etymology](/wiki/Etymology "Etymology") of the words *[valence](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/valence#Noun "wikt:valence")* (plural *[valences](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/valences#Noun "wikt:valences")*) and *[valency](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/valency#Noun "wikt:valency")* (plural *[valencies](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/valencies#Noun "wikt:valencies")*) traces back to 1425, meaning "extract, preparation", from Latin *valentia* "strength, capacity", from the earlier *valor* "worth, value", and the chemical meaning referring to the "combining power of an element" is recorded from 1884, from German *Valenz*.[[4]](#cite_note-4)
[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/18/Higgins-particles.jpg/500px-Higgins-particles.jpg)](/wiki/File:Higgins-particles.jpg)
[William Higgins](/wiki/William_Higgins_(chemist) "William Higgins (chemist)")' combinations of ultimate particles (1789)
The concept of valence was developed in the second half of the 19th century and helped successfully explain the molecular structure of inorganic and organic compounds.[[1]](#cite_note-Partington-1)
The quest for the underlying causes of valence led to the modern theories of chemical bonding, including the [cubical atom](/wiki/Cubical_atom "Cubical atom") (1902), [Lewis structures](/wiki/Lewis_structure "Lewis structure") (1916), [valence bond theory](/wiki/Valence_bond_theory "Valence bond theory") (1927), [molecular orbitals](/wiki/Molecular_orbital "Molecular orbital") (1928), [valence shell electron pair repulsion theory](/wiki/VSEPR_theory "VSEPR theory") (1958), and all of the advanced methods of [quantum chemistry](/wiki/Quantum_chemistry "Quantum chemistry").
In 1789, [William Higgins](/wiki/William_Higgins_(chemist) "William Higgins (chemist)") published views on what he called combinations of "ultimate" particles, which foreshadowed the concept of [valency bonds](/wiki/Valency_bonds "Valency bonds").[[5]](#cite_note-Partingtonhistory-5) If, for example, according to Higgins, the force between the ultimate particle of oxygen and the ultimate particle of nitrogen were 6, then the strength of the force would be divided accordingly, and likewise for the other combinations of ultimate particles (see illustration).
The exact inception, however, of the theory of chemical valencies can be traced to an 1852 paper by [Edward Frankland](/wiki/Edward_Frankland "Edward Frankland"), in which he combined the older [radical theory](/wiki/Radical_theory "Radical theory") with thoughts on [chemical affinity](/wiki/Chemical_affinity "Chemical affinity") to show that certain elements have the tendency to combine with other elements to form compounds containing 3, i.e., in the 3-atom groups (e.g., NO3, NH3, NI3, etc.) or 5, i.e., in the 5-atom groups (e.g., NO5, NH4O, PO5, etc.), equivalents of the attached elements. According to him, this is the manner in which their affinities are best satisfied, and by following these examples and postulates, he declares how obvious it is that[[6]](#cite_note-6)
.mw-parser-output .templatequote{overflow:hidden;margin:1em 0;padding:0 32px}.mw-parser-output .templatequotecite{line-height:1.5em;text-align:left;margin-top:0}@media(min-width:500px){.mw-parser-output .templatequotecite{padding-left:1.6em}}
> A tendency or law prevails (here), and that, no matter what the characters of the uniting atoms may be, the **combining power** of the attracting element, if I may be allowed the term, is always satisfied by the same number of these atoms.
This "combining power" was afterwards called **quantivalence** or valency (and valence by American chemists).[[5]](#cite_note-Partingtonhistory-5) In 1857 [August Kekulé](/wiki/August_Kekul%C3%A9 "August Kekulé") proposed fixed valences for many elements, such as 4 for carbon, and used them to propose [structural formulas](/wiki/Structural_formula "Structural formula") for many [organic](/wiki/Organic_chemistry "Organic chemistry") molecules, which are still accepted today.
[Lothar Meyer](/wiki/Lothar_Meyer "Lothar Meyer") in his 1864 book, *Die modernen Theorien der Chemie*, contained an early version of the periodic table containing 28 elements, for the first time classified elements into six families by their valence. Works on organizing the elements by [atomic weight](/wiki/Atomic_weight "Atomic weight"), until then had been stymied by the widespread use of [equivalent weights](/wiki/Equivalent_weight "Equivalent weight") for the elements, rather than atomic weights.[[7]](#cite_note-7)
Most 19th-century chemists defined the valence of an element as the number of its bonds without distinguishing different types of valence or of bond. However, in 1893 [Alfred Werner](/wiki/Alfred_Werner "Alfred Werner") described [transition metal](/wiki/Transition_metal "Transition metal") [coordination complexes](/wiki/Coordination_complex "Coordination complex") such as [Co(NH3)6]Cl3, in which he distinguished *principal* and *subsidiary* valences (German: 'Hauptvalenz' and 'Nebenvalenz'), corresponding to the modern concepts of [oxidation state](/wiki/Oxidation_state "Oxidation state") and [coordination number](/wiki/Coordination_number "Coordination number") respectively.
For [main-group elements](/wiki/Main-group_element "Main-group element"), in 1904 [Richard Abegg](/wiki/Richard_Abegg "Richard Abegg")

question: How many smoke grenade models listed in the Polish Land Forces equipment originate exclusively from the Soviet Union?

Military unit
The following is a **list of current equipment of the Polish Land Forces**. This is not to be confused with the equipment of the Polish Armed Forces, Navy, air force, or territorial defence forces. The list includes current equipment such as helmets, vision systems, communication systems, small arms, combat vehicles, transport vehicles, explosives, missile systems, engineering vehicles, logistical vehicles, trucks, artillery, air defence, helicopters, Unmanned aerial aircraft, as well as future equipment and equipment being replaced.
Personnel equipment
-------------------
[[edit](/w/index.php?title=List_of_equipment_of_the_Polish_Land_Forces&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Personnel equipment")]
### Helmets
[[edit](/w/index.php?title=List_of_equipment_of_the_Polish_Land_Forces&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Helmets")]
| Model | Image | Origin | Type | Variant | Quantity | Details |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **[Hełm HP-05](/wiki/He%C5%82m_HP-05 "Hełm HP-05")** |  | [Poland](/wiki/Poland "Poland") | [Combat helmet](/wiki/Combat_helmet "Combat helmet") | HP-05 | 50,000/93,000 | High cut type helmets. 50,000 delivered.
